steps.title: Identify the passage's alternative interpretation
steps.content: The passage presents two readings of Instant Mural: (1) the women-constraints reading, which explains Valdez’s presence but not Sandoval’s; and (2) the art-establishment reading, which explains both figures as Chicano artists constrained by aesthetic expectations. The passage explicitly states the alternative interpretation “accounts for all the evidence.” The correct answer must capture this broader, aesthetic-constraint framing.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'while Valdez’s presence in Instant Mural represents the social constraints placed on women at the time, Sandoval’s presence represents Chicano muralists’ frustration at their lack of recognition by the art establishment.': Retains the rejected women-constraints angle for Valdez and misidentifies what constrains Sandoval (recognition, not aesthetic expectations). ✗
  • Choice B — 'Instant Mural is best understood not as a critique of the social constraints placed on women but rather as a critique of sociohistorical muralists’ depictions of Chicano culture.': Replaces the women-constraints angle with a critique of muralist depictions — not what the alternative interpretation says. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the main subject of Instant Mural is female Chicano artists’ experience of being doubly constrained by gender-role expectations and the marginalization of certain types of art.': Reintroduces gender constraints, which the passage’s alternative interpretation moves away from. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Instant Mural is a reflection on the constraining aesthetic expectations placed on Chicano artists in general rather than on the social constraints placed on women specifically.': Correctly captures the alternative interpretation: both figures are constrained by aesthetic expectations placed on Chicano artists, not by gender roles. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
